steps.title: Identify the grammatical structure
steps.content: The blank contains a title (linguist) immediately followed by a proper name (Marian Klamer). The two are placed directly together with no intervening words—the name identifies which person the title refers to.

steps.title: Apply the punctuation rule for appositives
steps.content: When a title precedes a proper name that identifies the specific person (a restrictive appositive), no punctuation separates them. Linguist Marian Klamer correctly unites the title and identifying name in one phrase. Option A asymmetrically places a comma only after the title; option B uses commas around the name, incorrectly treating it as nonrestrictive information; option D uses a colon, which doesn't fit an appositive. That matches choice C — C is correct.
